title: strychnos brasiliensis var. normalis
intro: Strychnos brasiliensis var. normalis, also known as the poison nut, is a plant species found in the tropical forests of Brazil and other parts of South America. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits associated with Strychnos brasiliensis var. normalis include pain relief, reduction of fever, and treatment of various gastrointestinal issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used in folk medicine to alleviate various health conditions.
